mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through lower discount rates and higher risk appetite, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average during months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the sign flips and rising FEDFUNDS reliably precedes higher returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply above recent levels, equity investors price a higher recession probability and weaker earnings, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment jumps are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A broad contraction in real demand, captured by declining RSAFS and weakening GDPC1, signals softer nominal revenue growth and lower earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in retail sales and real GDP do not precede below-baseline 60-day returns, or if either series alone explains the signal equally well with no interaction gain.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial capacity utilization and better cyclical earnings momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained increases in INDPRO fail to associate with positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns in walk-forward samples, or if the sign is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts breakout above recent trend reflects improving credit transmission and household demand, which supports cyclical equity multiples and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts breakouts do not precede above-average 60-day returns, or if the signal is absent when HOUST crosses back above trend.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high CPIAUCSL inflation with an unchanged or rising FEDFUNDS rate raises real-rate pressure and tightens valuation multiples, so the joint inflation-rate squeeze predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS is flat or rising
failure_condition: Reject if inflation plus firm policy rates do not jointly improve downside prediction versus either series alone, or if triggered episodes show non-negative 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 returns when employment is expanding because stronger labor income supports consumption and earnings, but the effect should vanish when unemployment is rising at the same time.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is rising and UNRATE is not rising
failure_condition: Reject if payroll expansions do not outperform baseline over 5 trading days, or if the signal remains positive even when unemployment is clearly worsening.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates accelerating final demand and revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail-sales upswings do not produce positive 60-day returns more often than not, or if the combined signal offers no lift over single-series models.
